div.head: The Chargé in Brazil (Donnelly) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: With reference to the patrolling of the South Atlantic, I have the honor to enclose copy and translation of a self-explanatory communiquéCommuniqué not printed; it indicated that the patrolling of the South Atlantic was to be done not jointly with United States forces but by the Brazilian Navy alone. issued by the Minister of Marine of Brazil on this subject.

With respect to the convoying of merchant vessels, Admiral Ingram informed the Embassy that the Brazilian Navy was in charge of the last convoy from Brazilian ports to Trinidad. He said that henceforth the Brazilian Navy would be in charge of convoying merchant vessels on the Brazil-Trinidad section of the South Atlantic route.
div.closer:
For the Chargé d’Affaires a. i.:
Harold S. Tewell
First Secretary of Embassy
